description: Wi-Fi + Bluetooth SoC module, dual-core 240 MHz Xtensa LX6, 4MB flash, integrated antenna — the standard ESP32 module
frequency_range: Wi-Fi 2.4 GHz 802.11 b/g/n; Bluetooth 4.2 BR/EDR + BLE

absolute_max.vcc: 3.6V absolute maximum — exceeding this destroys the module
absolute_max.gpio_voltage: 3.3V max on all GPIO — 5V kills the chip
absolute_max.max_gpio_current: 12 mA source per GPIO, 28 mA sink per GPIO
absolute_max.total_current: Up to 500 mA peak during Wi-Fi TX bursts

key_performance.cpu: Dual-core Xtensa LX6, up to 240 MHz
key_performance.spi: 4× SPI (1 used for flash, 1 for PSRAM if present)

pins.name: GND
pins.description: Ground. Left side pin 1 (top-left corner).
pins.requirements: Connect to ground plane.

pins.name: 3V3
pins.description: 3.3V power supply input. Powers the entire module.
pins.requirements: MUST be 3.0–3.6V. Add 10µF + 100nF decoupling caps close to module. Supply must provide 500 mA peak during Wi-Fi bursts. Use LDO capable of 600mA+ (e.g., AMS1117-3.3 with 1A input supply).

pins.name: EN
pins.description: Chip enable. Active-high. Pull high to run; pull low to reset the module. Driven by external RC reset circuit.
pins.requirements: Add 10kΩ pull-up to 3V3 and 100nF cap to GND for RC reset. Can connect to DTR/RTS via transistor circuit for auto-reset during programming (same as Arduino).

pins.name: GPIO36/VP
pins.description: Input-only GPIO, ADC1_CH0. No output capability, no pull-up/pull-down hardware. Analog input 0–3.3V.
pins.requirements: Input only — do not drive with outputs expecting to source current. ADC input: 0–3.3V range.

pins.name: GPIO39/VN
pins.description: Input-only GPIO, ADC1_CH3. No output capability, no pull-up/pull-down hardware.
pins.requirements: Input only.

pins.name: GPIO34
pins.description: Input-only GPIO, ADC1_CH6. No output driver, no internal pull.
pins.requirements: Input only. Add external pull-up or pull-down if needed.

pins.name: GPIO35
pins.description: Input-only GPIO, ADC1_CH7. No output driver, no internal pull.
pins.requirements: Input only.

pins.name: GPIO32
pins.description: GPIO32, ADC1_CH4, XTAL32K_P (32 kHz crystal). Full I/O with internal pull-up/down.
pins.requirements: General GPIO, ADC, or 32.768 kHz crystal input.

pins.name: GPIO33
pins.description: GPIO33, ADC1_CH5, XTAL32K_N (32 kHz crystal). Full I/O.
pins.requirements: General GPIO, ADC, or 32.768 kHz crystal output.

pins.name: GPIO25
pins.description: GPIO25, ADC2_CH8, DAC1 output. 8-bit DAC.
pins.requirements: GPIO, ADC, or DAC output. ADC2 cannot be used while Wi-Fi is active.

pins.name: GPIO26
pins.description: GPIO26, ADC2_CH9, DAC2 output. 8-bit DAC.
pins.requirements: GPIO, ADC, or DAC output. ADC2 unavailable during Wi-Fi.

pins.name: GPIO27
pins.description: GPIO27, ADC2_CH7, I2S. General purpose I/O.
pins.requirements: General GPIO or ADC2 (no Wi-Fi when used as ADC).

pins.name: GPIO14
pins.description: GPIO14, ADC2_CH6, HSPI_CLK. Boot strapping pin — outputs PWM signal at boot.
pins.requirements: BOOT STRAPPING: must be pulled low or left floating at boot (if pulled high, chip enters SD card boot mode). Avoid driving external devices that interpret the boot-time pulse.

pins.name: GPIO12
pins.description: GPIO12, ADC2_CH5, HSPI_MISO. Boot strapping pin — selects flash voltage.
pins.requirements: CRITICAL BOOT STRAP: MUST be LOW at boot. If pulled high, flash is powered at 1.8V instead of 3.3V — this causes boot failure on standard modules. Never pull this high via external circuit. Use internal pull-down.

pins.name: GND
pins.description: Ground (center-left of module).
pins.requirements: Connect to ground plane.

pins.name: GPIO13
pins.description: GPIO13, ADC2_CH4, HSPI_MOSI, U0CTS. General purpose I/O.
pins.requirements: General GPIO.

pins.name: GPIO9/SD2
pins.description: GPIO9, connected to internal SPI flash SD_DATA2. DO NOT USE for general GPIO on standard modules.
pins.requirements: RESERVED — connected to embedded flash chip SPI bus. Using this pin as GPIO will corrupt flash reads and crash the firmware. Leave unconnected.

pins.name: GPIO10/SD3
pins.description: GPIO10, connected to internal SPI flash SD_DATA3. DO NOT USE.
pins.requirements: RESERVED — same as GPIO9. Leave unconnected.

pins.name: GPIO11/CMD
pins.description: GPIO11, connected to internal SPI flash CMD line. DO NOT USE.
pins.requirements: RESERVED — same as GPIO9. Leave unconnected.

pins.name: VDD_SDIO
pins.description: Supply for SDIO/SPI flash interface. Usually not exposed on DevKit boards; tie to 3V3 or leave per module design.
pins.requirements: On WROOM-32 module this is internally connected. Do not connect externally unless explicitly designing for PSRAM or alternative flash.

pins.name: GPIO6/CLK
pins.description: GPIO6, connected to internal SPI flash CLK. DO NOT USE.
pins.requirements: RESERVED — connected to embedded flash chip. Leave unconnected.

pins.name: GPIO7/SD0
pins.description: GPIO7, connected to internal SPI flash SD_DATA0. DO NOT USE.
pins.requirements: RESERVED — leave unconnected.

pins.name: GPIO8/SD1
pins.description: GPIO8, connected to internal SPI flash SD_DATA1. DO NOT USE.
pins.requirements: RESERVED — leave unconnected.

pins.name: GPIO15
pins.description: GPIO15, ADC2_CH3, HSPI_SS, U0RTS. Boot strapping pin.
pins.requirements: BOOT STRAP: internal pull-up makes this HIGH at boot (selects SDIO Slave boot). For normal SPI flash boot leave high (default) or tie high. If using as GPIO output: no issue after boot.

pins.name: GPIO2
pins.description: GPIO2, ADC2_CH2. Boot strapping pin — must be low or floating during flashing.
pins.requirements: BOOT STRAP: must be LOW during programming (to enter download mode). On DevKit boards this pin connects to the onboard LED via 1kΩ resistor. If driving external devices, ensure GPIO2 is not held HIGH during flashing or the chip will not enter boot mode.

pins.name: GPIO0
pins.description: GPIO0, ADC2_CH1, CLK_OUT1. Boot strapping pin — determines boot mode.
pins.requirements: BOOT STRAP: HIGH = run from flash (normal operation). LOW = download/programming mode. On DevKit a BOOT button pulls this low. External circuit: 10kΩ pull-up to 3V3; add button to GND for manual flashing. Do not hold low after power-on.

pins.name: GPIO4
pins.description: GPIO4, ADC2_CH0, touch sensor T0. Full I/O with capacitive touch.
pins.requirements: General GPIO or capacitive touch input.

pins.name: GPIO16
pins.description: GPIO16, U2RXD. Often used as UART2 RX or PSRAM clock on modules with PSRAM.
pins.requirements: General GPIO or UART2 RX. On modules with PSRAM (WROVER), GPIO16/17 are reserved for PSRAM — check module variant.

pins.name: GPIO17
pins.description: GPIO17, U2TXD. UART2 TX.
pins.requirements: General GPIO or UART2 TX. Reserved on PSRAM modules.

pins.name: GPIO5
pins.description: GPIO5, VSPI_SS. Boot strapping pin — controls SDIO timing.
pins.requirements: BOOT STRAP: must be HIGH at boot (default — internal pull-up). Leave high or add pull-up if using as SPI SS.

pins.name: GPIO18
pins.description: GPIO18, VSPI_CLK. SPI clock for VSPI bus.
pins.requirements: General GPIO or VSPI CLK.

pins.name: GPIO19
pins.description: GPIO19, VSPI_MISO, U0CTS.
pins.requirements: General GPIO or VSPI MISO.

pins.name: GPIO21
pins.description: GPIO21, I2C SDA (default). Recommended pin for I2C data line.
pins.requirements: For I2C: add 4.7kΩ pull-up to 3V3. Open-drain bus.

pins.name: GPIO3/RXD0
pins.description: GPIO3, UART0 RX. Default serial RX for programming and Serial.print() output.
pins.requirements: UART0 RX — connected to USB-Serial adapter on DevKit boards for programming. Avoid using as GPIO if UART0 is needed.

pins.name: GPIO1/TXD0
pins.description: GPIO1, UART0 TX. Default serial TX for programming and debug output.
pins.requirements: UART0 TX — connected to USB-Serial adapter on DevKit boards. Outputs boot log messages at 115200 baud on power-up — avoid if clean 3.3V logic line is needed at startup.

pins.name: GPIO22
pins.description: GPIO22, I2C SCL (default), VSPI_WP.
pins.requirements: For I2C: add 4.7kΩ pull-up to 3V3.

pins.name: GPIO23
pins.description: GPIO23, VSPI_MOSI.
pins.requirements: General GPIO or VSPI MOSI.

pins.name: GND
pins.description: Ground (bottom-right of module).
pins.requirements: Connect to ground plane.

required_passives.reason: Bulk decoupling for 500mA Wi-Fi TX current bursts — prevents brownout reset
EN pull-up to keep module running. Required.
RC reset on EN — ensures clean power-on reset
GPIO0 boot strap pull-up — ensures normal boot mode. Add push-button to GND for programming mode.

application_circuits.name: Minimal ESP32-WROOM-32 circuit
application_circuits.description: 3V3 supply with 10µF + 100nF decoupling. EN with 10kΩ pull-up + 100nF to GND for RC reset. GPIO0 with 10kΩ pull-up + BOOT button to GND. UART0 (TX/RX) connected to USB-Serial (CH340/CP2102). GPIO6-11 left unconnected (internal flash). All boot-strap pins at correct levels.

common_mistakes: 5V on ANY pin — the ESP32 is 3.3V ONLY. All GPIO and supply are 3.3V max. Connecting to 5V Arduino directly kills the chip.
GPIO6, GPIO7, GPIO8, GPIO9, GPIO10, GPIO11 are connected to the internal flash chip — using them as GPIO corrupts flash and crashes firmware.
GPIO12 must be LOW at boot — if pulled HIGH, flash voltage is set to 1.8V, causing immediate boot failure.
ADC2 (GPIO0,2,4,12–15,25–27) cannot be used while Wi-Fi radio is active — ADC reads return incorrect values.
GPIO34, GPIO35, GPIO36, GPIO39 are INPUT ONLY — no output driver, no internal pull resistors.
The 3.3V supply must handle 500 mA peak during Wi-Fi TX — small LDOs (100 mA) will brownout. Use AMS1117-3.3 (1A) or better.
EN pin requires proper RC reset circuit — without 100nF cap, module may fail to start reliably on power-up.
GPIO1 (TXD0) outputs boot messages at 115200 baud on every power-up — do not connect to a device that cannot tolerate this.
GPIO16/GPIO17 are reserved for PSRAM on ESP32-WROVER variant — check module type before using these pins.
I2C requires external 4.7kΩ pull-ups to 3.3V on SDA (GPIO21) and SCL (GPIO22) — internal pull-ups are too weak for reliable operation.
notes: ESP32-WROOM-32 is a certified module (FCC/CE/IC) — use the module, not bare ESP32 chip, for compliance. Programming: USB-Serial adapter connected to GPIO1(TX) and GPIO3(RX). Auto-reset circuit: EN and GPIO0 driven by DTR/RTS via transistors. Datasheet: Espressif ESP32-WROOM-32 Datasheet v3.2.
